claim.content: The Kadowaki–Woods (KW) ratio is defined as A/γ_0^2, where A is the coefficient of the low-temperature resistivity ρ(T) = ρ_0 + A T^2 and γ_0 is the Sommerfeld coefficient from C(T) = γ_0 T in the Fermi-liquid regime; the proposition is that an observed decrease of A/γ_0^2 with increasing arsenic substitution, for the experimentally measured A and γ_0 values, reflects a genuine reduction of effective-mass renormalization and many-body electronic correlations (i.e., a crossover from heavy-fermion to more weakly correlated metallic behaviour), even though possible concurrent changes in quasiparticle degeneracy N or detailed band-structure factors are not explicitly accounted for in the simple ratio.
